$comment: Documented-EXCEPTION ledger for the parity closed loop. SOURCE OF TRUTH = the shots + specs generated from the mock design; any visual divergence from them is a BUG to FIX by default. This ledger holds ONLY the narrow, documented exceptions where the FE intentionally does NOT match the mock, each governed by a doc — it is NOT a 'redesign' escape hatch. Valid exception kinds: 'behavior' (docs/business win behaviour over the mock's visual — e.g. study flip-card), 'future'/'rejected' (feature scope per docs), 'needs-schema' (data not in the read model yet). A finding that MATCHES an entry → exception (cite source); anything not listed → FIX. Match: screen == entry.screen AND (entry.node == '*' OR node startsWith entry.node) AND (entry.kind == '*' OR entry.kind == finding kind). source MUST cite a doc/owner ruling. Keep this MINIMAL — when in doubt, leave it out and fix the FE to match the mock.

$example.reason: FE uses swipe/both-sides per study-flow; the kit flip-card is a documented behaviour-owned visual gap, not a fix.

exceptions.reason: FE adds a front-side TTS speaker button (mx-node:study-session/speak) authorized by WBS 8.4.3; the kit shots carry no speaker affordance. Hidden when the deck target_language is unsupported, so its absence-from-design is intentional.
Front-side TTS speaker (WBS 8.4.3) added to the prompt card; not in the kit shots. Hidden for unsupported-language decks.
Front-side TTS speaker (WBS 8.4.3) added to the prompt card (realizes the wireframe S65 TTS affordance); not in the kit shots. Hidden for unsupported-language decks.
Front-side TTS speaker (WBS 8.4.3) added only on the REVEALED correct/wrong answer card (Fill never speaks the front while it is the hidden answer, and does not auto-play); not in the kit shots.

The kit tags a deck-selector (deck retargeting), but that is Future (§V1); the FE maps its single 'Note' to the Details fields (example/pronunciation/hint) and does not build a picker. No FE key / parity assertion until deck retargeting ships.
Same as 07 — the deck-selector is Future (deck retargeting); the FE edit form has no picker.

The kit footer has a manual 'Next' pill; the FE guess mode AUTO-ADVANCES after an answer (depleting-bar footer, tappable to skip) per study-flow, so there is no manual primary-action button to key. study-session/action is keyed in recall (Show answer) + fill (Check) where the mode has a manual action.

The kit footnote reads 'Changing the language restarts the app.', but the FE wires MaterialApp.locale reactively (AppLanguageX.locale) so the app re-localizes LIVE with no restart. Copy resolved toward accurate behavior ('Changes apply across the app right away.'); documented in nav-flow/overview/WBS. The kit shot/spec keep the original copy (not regenerated) — same mock↔behavior pattern as the study flip-card example.
exceptions.source: lib/app/memox_app.dart (MaterialApp.locale wiring) + docs/business/navigation/navigation-flow.md (/settings/language live re-localize) + lib/l10n/app_en.arb (languageRestartNote description)

exceptions.reason: Mock vs doc conflict, resolved toward the doc (CLAUDE.md). The kit PNG shows a top-right close-x, but wireframe 18 specifies ONE exit — the body 'Done' button (mx-node:17-study-result/done-button) — with a deliberate nav contract: Done uses `go` to the SCOPE-AWARE origin (deck -> that deck's list, else Dashboard), the result screen is NOT kept in the back stack, and re-entry is forbidden. A second close-x would be redundant and would tempt an inconsistent exit that breaks that origin-routing/no-re-entry contract. Hence no FE key. Re-evaluate only if wireframe 18 adds a distinct dismiss affordance.
exceptions.source: docs/wireframes/18-study-result.md:165 (Done MUST use go, not stacked) + §Agent rule (origin routing) + lib/presentation/features/study/screens/study_result_screen.dart:165 (_done)

exceptions.reason: The mock shows distinct per-deck glyphs/colours, but decks carry no stored icon/colour (only folders do). FE uses a generic deck glyph (Icons.style_outlined) + cycles the four SRS-status tints by row index. A real per-deck icon/colour needs a decks.icon/decks.color migration (Q1).
Per-deck mastery rows reuse the generic deck glyph + cycled tints (see deck-icon) — same schema gap (Q1).

The mock goal slider exposes 5..60; the LearningSettings contract validates dailyNewLimit 5..200 (step 5). FE slider follows the mock's 5..60 (mock wins visual); validation is unchanged (docs win behavior) so a larger persisted value stays valid but unreachable from the slider (Q9).

The mock System row subtitle shows the resolved device locale ('English (United States)'); resolving + pretty-printing the live device locale is out of scope. FE shows a static localized 'Match device language'. Resolved-locale display name is Future (Q11).
exceptions.source: kit 25 system row; no device-locale display-name resolver in scope

exceptions.reason: The kit partial-result primary action is 'Review skipped' (a dedicated skipped-rows review); no such surface exists. FE uses 'Import another file' (restart); skipped rows are already shown in the pre-commit preview. A post-commit skipped-review screen is Future (Q7).
exceptions.source: kit 10 partial mock; no review surface in scope

exceptions.reason: The mock mentions Anki .apkg + a live 'N of M imported' counter; neither is buildable. FE accepts csv/tsv/txt only (CSV parser can't read .apkg zip/sqlite) and the importing state uses a plain MxLoadingState (commitDeckImport is one atomic batch, no progress events). Both Future (Q8).
TTS slider visual range follows the mock where it doesn't break validation, but the engine uses the CONTRACT range (rate 0.3..0.7) — a real engine constraint (locked TTS-23 decision).
TTS pitch slider uses the contract engine range (pitch 0.7..1.5) — a real engine constraint (locked TTS-23 decision).
The mock's preview waveform animates; FE renders a static accent waveform (no animation) — C4 polish, Future (locked TTS-23 decision).
The mock shows per-voice metadata; FE lists voices without extra metadata — C2 Future (locked TTS-23 decision).
The mock shows a skeleton loading state; FE uses a generic MxLoadingState — C3 Future (locked TTS-23 decision).
The mock implies an install-voices action; FE treats it as a no-op/Future (no voice-download flow) (locked TTS-23 decision).

$coverageExempt: Coverage-side ledger, consumed by tool/parity/mxnode_coverage.mjs. Lists kit CANDIDATE nodes that are intentionally NOT given a data-mx-node id, so the coverage report does not count them as gaps and `--check --min` does not trip on them. DISTINCT from `exceptions` (which is FE-differs-from-mock, for the parity test). Each entry is still a documented deviation from the FIX/TAG-by-default rule. Kinds: 'deferred' (the kit layout is PRE-redesign / stale vs the FE — tag once the kit is regenerated to the redesign on Claude Design), 'future'/'rejected' (the element is not built in the FE by scope/owner ruling), 'covered' (a decorative child INSIDE an already-tagged container — tag the container, never its inner parts, same rule as list items). Match: screen == entry.screen AND candidate.node startsWith entry.node AND (entry.mx absent OR entry.mx == candidate.mx). `source` MUST cite a doc / owner ruling. Keep MINIMAL — when in doubt, tag the node instead of exempting it.

coverageExempt.reason: The 'Recent decks' section header (overline + Library link) is part of the tagged `02-dashboard/recent-decks` section, rendered inside DashboardRecentDecks. Tag the section (the list card), not its header — same rule as 'tag the list, not each item'. MxSectionHeader has no dedicated FE component yet (componentGap).
coverageExempt.source: docs/system-design/MemoX Design System/CLAUDE.md (tag meaningful required nodes, not every div) + lib/presentation/features/dashboard/widgets/dashboard_recent_decks.dart

coverageExempt.reason: The 'Shuffle & restart' footer button exists in the kit but the FE match mode intentionally does not build it (mock-authoritative owner ruling: dropped affordances are Rejected/Future). Tag when/if the FE implements it.
coverageExempt.source: owner ruling 2026-06-22 (redesign mock authoritative; dropped wireframe affordances Rejected)

coverageExempt.reason: Decorative leading tile INSIDE the tagged deck picker (flashcard-editor/deck-picker). Tag the container, not its inner tile — same rule as 'tag the list, not each item'.
coverageExempt.source: docs/system-design/MemoX Design System/CLAUDE.md (common-layer first; tag meaningful required nodes, not every div)

coverageExempt.reason: The app-bar back arrow is the shared MxAppBar AUTOMATIC leading button (platform back), not a screen-specific affordance — the FE never hand-builds or keys it. Same rationale as 09-flashcard-history: the empty base state's app bar carries only the back arrow, so this lone icon-btn surfaces as a singleton (the file-chip's clear-× lives in later states).
